name: bundling
context: Bundle frontend apps for production: Vite, esbuild, webpack, tree-shaking, code splitting
info: This skill covers bundling frontend applications for production deployment. Vite is the recommended bundler — it provides instant dev server startup with native ES modules and optimized Rollup-based production builds. esbuild handles transpilation and minification at extreme speed. The guide covers code splitting (route-based and component-based), tree-shaking to eliminate dead code, minification, asset optimization (images, fonts), and output analysis. Webpack remains relevant for legacy projects. Build output should be deployed to edge platforms like Vercel or Cloudflare — the bundler produces static assets and optionally server-side rendering bundles.
body:
# Frontend Bundling

Bundling transforms your source code (TypeScript, JSX, CSS) into optimized assets for production. The bundler resolves imports, tree-shakes unused code, splits bundles for lazy loading, minifies output, and generates hashed filenames for cache busting.

## Vite

Vite is the recommended bundler for modern frontend projects:

```typescript
// vite.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import react from "@vitejs/plugin-react";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  build: {
    target: "es2022",
    outDir: "dist",
    sourcemap: true,
    rollupOptions: {
      output: {
        manualChunks: {
          vendor: ["react", "react-dom"],
          query: ["@tanstack/react-query"],
        },
      },
    },
  },
  css: {
    modules: {
      localsConvention: "camelCase",
    },
  },
});
```

Vite features:
- **Dev server** — native ES modules, instant HMR, no bundling in development
- **Production build** — Rollup-based, optimized chunks, CSS extraction
- **CSS** — PostCSS, Tailwind, CSS Modules built in (see [[frontend/css]])
- **TypeScript** — transpiles via esbuild, type checking via `tsc --noEmit`
- **Plugins** — React, Vue, Svelte, PWA, compression, and more

## esbuild

esbuild is an extremely fast bundler/minifier written in Go:

```bash
# Bundle a library
esbuild src/index.ts --bundle --outdir=dist --format=esm --minify --sourcemap

# Minify only
esbuild dist/bundle.js --minify --outfile=dist/bundle.min.js
```

Vite uses esbuild internally for:
- TypeScript and JSX transpilation in dev
- Dependency pre-bundling (node_modules)
- CSS and JS minification in production

## Code Splitting

Code splitting loads only the code needed for the current route or interaction:

```tsx
import { lazy, Suspense } from "react";

// Route-based splitting
const Dashboard = lazy(() => import("./pages/Dashboard"));
const Settings = lazy(() => import("./pages/Settings"));

function App() {
  return (
    <Suspense fallback={<Loading />}>
      <Routes>
        <Route path="/dashboard" element={<Dashboard />} />
        <Route path="/settings" element={<Settings />} />
      </Routes>
    </Suspense>
  );
}

// Component-based splitting for heavy components
const Chart = lazy(() => import("./components/Chart"));
```

Dynamic `import()` creates a split point. The bundler generates a separate chunk that loads on demand. Use `<Suspense>` to show a fallback while loading.

## Tree-Shaking

Tree-shaking eliminates unused exports from the final bundle:

- Use **named exports** instead of default exports — see [[frontend/react#components-jsx]]
- Use **ES modules** (`import`/`export`), not CommonJS (`require`/`module.exports`)
- Mark packages as **side-effect-free** in `package.json`: `"sideEffects": false`
- Vite and Rollup tree-shake automatically in production builds
- Tailwind CSS purges unused utility classes at build time — see [[frontend/css#tailwind]]

## Optimization

| Technique | Impact | Tool |
|-----------|--------|------|
| Minification | 50-70% size reduction | esbuild / Terser |
| Tree-shaking | Remove unused exports | Rollup / esbuild |
| Code splitting | Load only what's needed | Dynamic import() |
| Compression | 70-90% transfer reduction | gzip / Brotli |
| Image optimization | 40-80% image size reduction | sharp / squoosh |
| Font subsetting | Load only used glyphs | fonttools |
| CSS purging | Remove unused CSS | Tailwind / PurgeCSS |

Analyze bundle size with `rollup-plugin-visualizer` or `source-map-explorer`:

```bash
# Add to vite.config.ts plugins
import { visualizer } from "rollup-plugin-visualizer";
plugins: [react(), visualizer({ open: true })]

# Or use source-map-explorer
npx source-map-explorer dist/assets/*.js
```

## Build Commands

```bash
# Vite
npm run build          # vite build
npm run preview        # vite preview (serve production build locally)

# Next.js
npm run build          # next build
npm run start          # next start

# Custom
VITE_API_URL=https://api.example.com npm run build
```

sections.title: Vite
sections.body: Recommended bundler. Native ES modules in dev for instant startup and HMR. Rollup-based production builds with optimized chunking. Built-in support for TypeScript, JSX, CSS Modules, PostCSS, and Tailwind (see [[frontend/css#tailwind]]). Configure via vite.config.ts. Use manualChunks for vendor splitting. Plugin ecosystem for React, compression, PWA.

sections.title: esbuild
sections.body: Extremely fast Go-based bundler and minifier. Used internally by Vite for TypeScript/JSX transpilation and dependency pre-bundling. Can be used standalone for library bundling. Supports ESM and CJS output formats. Limited plugin API compared to Rollup but 10-100x faster.

sections.title: Code splitting
sections.body: Dynamic import() creates split points. Route-based splitting with React.lazy() and Suspense. Component-based splitting for heavy widgets (charts, editors, maps). Vite generates separate chunks loaded on demand. Use manualChunks in Rollup config for vendor bundle control. Next.js splits by page automatically.

sections.title: Tree-shaking
sections.body: Eliminates unused exports from bundles. Requires ES modules (import/export). Named exports tree-shake better than default exports — see [[frontend/react#components-jsx]]. Mark packages as side-effect-free in package.json. Tailwind purges unused CSS automatically (see [[frontend/css#tailwind]]). Rollup and esbuild handle tree-shaking in production.

sections.title: Build optimization
sections.body: Minification (esbuild/Terser) reduces code size 50-70%. Compression (gzip/Brotli) reduces transfer size 70-90%. Image optimization with sharp or squoosh. Font subsetting for used glyphs only. Bundle analysis with rollup-plugin-visualizer or source-map-explorer. Target modern browsers with build.target in Vite config.

BUILD_COMMAND.description: Build command to execute (default: npm run build)
